##################

#Data delivery by Nanopore of Micromonas commoda virus
#Virus strain used for Lucien Weymiens' experiment in virus and coevolution conditions
#The sequence here is the "virus t0 sequence"

##################

#02/07/2024
@ DATA DELIVERY OF McV 
Resequencing was done by Nanopore on McV by Aurelie Claes from DNA extracted by Thomas Anicet.


Bonjour à tous,

Le séquençage MinION est terminé et vous pourrez trouvez les résultats en cliquant sur le lien ci-dessous :

https://nuage.obs-banyuls.fr/s/59yHLotGs3Takk5

Bonne journée

Aurélie
barcode 	sample ID
06 	MPV
07 	N3
08 	N6
09 	N9
10 	P19
11 	S50-2
12 	S50-7
13 	S50-9
14 	T14-12
15 	T14-23
16 	T14-25

=====

SHEREE

Downloaded from Nuage the folder barcode06-MPV/
Containing 358 fastq.gz files.

Created a Temporary Reference Genome (TRG) -> described in sheree_README_McV_Nanopore_sequencing.txt

=====

LOU : 

Stored in folder virus_TRG


Goal 1 : Recreate the TRG by following the same steps as Sheree


#05/01/2026
@PREPARE DATA

Download the folder barcode06-MPV.zip
Containing 358 fastq.gz files.

Put in on Genotoul server
% put /Users/loudodin/Documents/Bioinformatics/virus_TRG/barcode06-MPV.zip

Unzip all of them
% unzip barcode06-MPV.zip 
It creates the folder barcode06-MPV/

Concatenate into one big fast
% cat barcode06-MPV/* >> virus_TRG_allfastq.fastq

Count reads
% grep -c "@" virus_TRG_allfastq.fastq
---> 158477 fastq reads

Double check
% grep '@' barcode06-MPV/* | wc -l
---> 158477 fastq reads



#05/01/2026
@ASSEMBLE THE READS


Script virus_TRG_scriptAssembly directly created inside ssh using Vi
===
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -p workq
#SBATCH -t 01-00:00:00 
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task 4
#SBATCH --mem=40G

module load bioinfo/Flye/2.9.2

flye --nano-raw virus_TRG_allfastq.fastq --out-dir virus_TRG_assembly --genome-size 250k --meta  --threads 4
===

% sbatch virus_TRG_scriptAssembly

---> Worked in 10 minutes
---> Folder virus_TRG_assembly

% more assembly_info.txt
===
#seq_name	length	cov.	circ.	repeat	mult.	alt_group	graph_path
contig_1	212176	770	N	N	4	*	1,2,2,2,2,2,2,2,3,-2,-2,-2,-2,-2,-2,-2
contig_6	29793	3	N	N	1	*	*,6,*
contig_11	29338	4	N	N	1	*	*,11,*
contig_81	26602	3	N	N	1	*	*,81,*
contig_31	26386	4	N	N	1	*	*,31,*
contig_64	24919	3	N	N	1	*	*,64,*
contig_36	20156	3	N	N	1	*	*,36,*
contig_80	20002	3	N	N	1	*	*,80,*
contig_40	19692	3	N	N	1	*	*,40,*
contig_20	16402	3	N	N	1	*	*,20,*
contig_25	16318	3	N	N	1	*	*,25,*
contig_76	15638	4	N	N	1	*	*,76,*
contig_72	15450	4	N	N	1	*	*,72,*
contig_83	14038	5	N	N	1	*	*,83,*
contig_8	13250	3	N	N	1	*	*,8,*
contig_51	12526	3	N	N	1	*	*,51,*
contig_41	12328	3	N	N	1	*	*,41,*
contig_9	12275	4	N	N	1	*	*,9,*
contig_5	11189	4	N	N	1	*	*,5,*
contig_50	10725	4	N	N	1	*	*,50,*
contig_61	10598	4	N	N	1	*	*,61,*
contig_84	10366	5	N	N	1	*	*,84,*
contig_28	9040	3	N	N	1	*	*,28,*
contig_66	8414	3	N	N	1	*	*,66,*
contig_45	8391	3	N	N	1	*	*,45,*
contig_74	8389	4	N	N	1	*	*,74,*
contig_75	8363	4	N	N	1	*	*,75,*
contig_13	8310	3	N	N	1	*	*,13,*
contig_38	7389	3	N	N	1	*	*,38,*
contig_73	7149	3	N	N	1	*	*,73,*
...
===

---> It is different than the one of Sheree ! But just different each time you launch it I think, there is some random when you make tries to find the best graph

I assume that the longest contig with the highest coverage contig_1 is the McV genome, as it has coverage >700x while the others have only 10s of coverage.


#22/01/26

medaka.sh
===
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -p workq
#SBATCH -t 01-00:00:00 
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task 4
#SBATCH --mem=40G

module load bioinfo/Minimap/2-2.28
module load bioinfo/samtools/1.21
module load bioinfo/Bcftools/1.9
module load bioinfo/Medaka/1.11.3

medaka_consensus -d virus_TRG_assembly/assembly.fasta -i virus_TRG_allfastq.fastq -o virus_TRG_assembly_medaka -b 25 -t 6
===

#06/01/26
Download on my MacBook to be able to use seqtk
% get assembly.fasta 


Install seqtk on my MacBook
===
1) Install Homebrew
% /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

2) Check Homebrew
% brew --version
---> Homebrew 5.0.9

3) Install seqtk
% brew install seqtk
===


Extract the contig_1 with seqtk
% echo "contig_1" > list_contig.txt
% seqtk subseq assembly.fasta list_contig.txt > virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta
---> Creates file virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta 
===
>contig_1

===






Goal 2 : Run tests to "quantify" if TRG is good


#06/01/26
@Annotate TRG genome using Galaxy -> PROKKA

Provide virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta 
Choose Kingdom : Viruses

Launched
---> History PROKKA Viruses

.txt
===
organism: Genus species strain 
contigs: 1
bases: 212176
CDS: 302
tRNA: 6
===

.err
===
Discrepancy Report Results

Summary
FATAL: MISSING_PROTEIN_ID:302 proteins have invalid IDs.
DISC_SOURCE_QUALS_ASNDISC:strain (all present, all unique)
DISC_SOURCE_QUALS_ASNDISC:taxname (all present, all unique)
DISC_FEATURE_COUNT:CDS: 302 present
DISC_FEATURE_COUNT:tRNA: 6 present
DISC_COUNT_NUCLEOTIDES:1 nucleotide Bioseqs are present
FEATURE_LOCATION_CONFLICT:308 features have inconsistent gene locations.
DISC_QUALITY_SCORES:Quality scores are missing on all sequences.
ONCALLER_COMMENT_PRESENT:1 comment descriptors were found (all same)
SHORT_PROT_SEQUENCES:5 protein sequences are shorter than 50 aa.
MISSING_GENOMEASSEMBLY_COMMENTS:1 bioseqs are missing GenomeAssembly structured comments
MOLTYPE_NOT_MRNA:1 molecule types are not set as mRNA.
TECHNIQUE_NOT_TSA:1 technique are not set as TSA
MISSING_STRUCTURED_COMMENT:1 sequences do not include structured comments.
MISSING_PROJECT:303 sequences do not include project.
DISC_INCONSISTENT_MOLINFO_TECH:Molinfo Technique Report (some missing, all same)
...
===

I block here for the moment



#06/01/26
@Map reads on TRG to see if we obtain normal distribution

Put virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta on Genotoul server
% put /Users/loudodin/Documents/Bioinformatics/virus_TRG/virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta 

Index TRG
% search_module bwa
% module load bioinfo/bwa-mem2/2.2.1
% bwa-mem2 index virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta
---> virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta.0123, .amb, .ann, .bwt.2bit.64, .pac

Map reads on TRG
% bwa-mem2 mem virus_TRG_assembly_contig1.fasta virus_TRG_allfastq.fastq -o virus_readsAlignedOnTRG.sam


Doesn't work


#12/01/26
